vulnerability.title: System Audit Logs Must Have Mode 0640 or Less Permissive
vulnerability.description: Determine where the audit logs are stored with the following command: $ sudo grep -iw log_file /etc/audit/auditd.conf log_file = /var/log/audit/audit.log Configure the audit log to be protected from unauthorized read access by setting the correct permissive mode with the following command: $ sudo chmod 0600 audit_log_file By default, audit_log_file is "/var/log/audit/audit.log".
vulnerability.recommendation: Remediation script: # Remediation is applicable only in certain platforms if rpm --quiet -q audit && rpm --quiet -q kernel-core; then if LC_ALL=C grep -iw ^log_file /etc/audit/auditd.conf; then FILE=$(awk -F "=" '/^log_file/ {print $2}' /etc/audit/auditd.conf | tr -d ' ') else FILE="/var/log/audit/audit.log" fi chmod 0600 $FILE else >&2 echo 'Remediation is not applicable, nothing was done' fi

triage_decision.reason: The issue is a file permission setting for the audit log. Changing the mode to 0600 is a non‑intrusive fix that does not affect service operation or access mechanisms, making it safe to remediate automatically.
triage_decision.estimated_impact: filesystems affected; audit log file path; potential misconfiguration if path is overridden

review_feedback: Review+QA approved but the verification scan failed. Try a different approach.

concerns: The script does not restart or reload the auditd service after changing permissions, which may leave the daemon writing to a file with old permissions until restart.
Log rotation configurations may overwrite the 0600 mode, requiring explicit adjustment of logrotate settings.
Hard‑coding chmod 0600 may be overly restrictive if a dedicated audit group is preferred (0640) for authorized read access.
suggested_improvements: Add a service restart (systemctl restart auditd or service auditd restart) after chmod to ensure the new mode is used.
Check and update logrotate configuration to create the log file with mode 0600 (or 0640 with appropriate group).
Consider using a dedicated group (e.g., 'audit') and set mode 0640 if legitimate non‑root readers need access, with proper group ownership.
